entry_conditions.value: Execution starts at START node leading directly to A7.

exit_conditions.value: Execution reaches END node via X1 or V3.
exit_conditions.source_text:
X1 -> END [continuation not justified]
V3 -> END

required_steps.value: Node A7: Expands one state into multiple paths.
required_steps.source_text: - A7 expands one state into multiple paths.

required_steps.value: Nodes K2, M9, R4: Parallel or alternate path elements branches out from A7 and converging into T6.
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether K2, M9, and R4 execute concurrently, contextually, or as disjoint options is not specified by the graph structure.

required_steps.value: Node T6: Adds a differentiating input.
Node X1: Re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.
required_steps.source_text: - X1 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.

optional_steps.value: Node Q8: Reopens continuation when separation remains weak.
optional_steps.source_text: - Q8 reopens continuation when separation remains weak.
optional_steps.ambiguity: Executed conditionally based on X1 transition threshold.

optional_steps.value: Node V3: Closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.
optional_steps.source_text: - V3 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.
optional_steps.ambiguity: Executed conditionally based on X1 path selection.

loops.value: Loop from X1 through Q8 back to K2, M9, and R4.
loops.source_text:
X1 -> Q8 [threshold not met]
Q8 -> K2
Q8 -> M9
Q8 -> R4

branches.value: Branching from A7 to K2, M9, R4.
branches.ambiguity: Exact branching semantics (parallel vs. exclusive OR) are unstated.

branches.value: Conditional branching from X1 to Q8, V3, or END.
branches.source_text:
X1 -> Q8 [threshold not met]
X1 -> V3 [one path selected]
X1 -> END [continuation not justified]

termination_conditions.value: One path remains strongest after re-sorting at V3.

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Divergent-convergent block leading to a three-way conditional switch, with one branch forming a feedback loop back to the divergent paths.

primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: Conditional branching and loop feedback based on state evaluations.

stopping_criteria.value: Continuation is not justified during path fit evaluation.
One path becomes clearly strongest, allowing graph closure.

body.description: Expands one state into multiple paths
Re-sorts path fit after the new input appears
Reopens continuation when separation remains weak
Closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins by expanding a single state into multiple paths via node A7. These paths are processed across elements K2, M9, and R4, which then converge at node T6 where a differentiating input is added. Node X1 evaluates the paths by re-sorting their fit given the new input. Depending on the evaluation at X1, the procedure branches three ways: if continuation is not justified, execution terminates directly; if one path is selected as the strongest, node V3 closes the process and it ends; if the threshold is not met due to weak separation, node Q8 reopens continuation and loops back to K2, M9, and R4 for further cycling.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: START -> A7 -> [K2, M9, R4] -> T6 -> X1 -> {IF continuation not justified -> END | IF one path selected -> V3 -> END | IF threshold not met -> Q8 -> [K2, M9, R4]}

plausible_interpretations: The three target nodes are executed concurrently.
The system selects a subset or a single path based on internal conditions not explicitly listed.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects the structural categorization of the primitive sequence between A7 and T6.

notes: The procedure maps out a classical dynamic refinement loop or beam-search-style evaluation graph with explicit termination and feedback conditions.
